truthSpec.title: Config File Migration
truthSpec.overview: Migrate YAML configuration files from v1 format to v2 format, with specific restructuring rules.

requirements.text: Accept a v1 YAML config string and return a v2 YAML config string
Nest database-related keys (host, port, name, user, password) under services.database
Rename log_level to logging.verbosity (nested under logging key)
Set version: 2 at the root level
Preserve all other top-level keys unchanged

edgeCases: v1 config may not have all database keys — migrate only those present
log_level may be missing — omit logging.verbosity if so
Extra unknown keys should be preserved at root level
Handle numeric values correctly (port should remain a number)
truthSpec.testSummary: 5 tests covering: basic migration, database nesting, log_level renaming, version field, and preservation of extra keys.

aiBrief.title: Config File Migration
aiBrief.overview: Migrate YAML configuration files from v1 format to v2 format with restructuring and validation.
requirements: Accept a v1 YAML config string and return a v2 YAML config string
Nest database keys under database.connection (host, port, name, user, password)
Rename log_level to logging.level (nested under logging key)
Set version: 2 at the root level
Validate that all required database keys are present and throw if any are missing

flaws.description: Brief nests under database.connection; truth nests under services.database
Brief renames to logging.level; truth renames to logging.verbosity
Brief adds validation requirement that doesn't exist in truth — truth says migrate only present keys
Brief assumes comments should be stripped; truth says nothing about comments
